However, it is not routinely recorded in structured electronic health records. Here, we present a generalizable method for the automated classification of TNM stage directly from pathology report text. We train a BERT-based model using publicly available pathology reports across approximately 7,000 patients and 23 cancer types. We explore the use of different model types, with differing input sizes, parameters, and model architectures. Our final model goes beyond term-extraction, inferring TNM stage from context when it is not included in the report text explicitly. As external validation, we test our model on almost 8,000 pathology reports from Columbia University Medical Center, finding that our trained model achieved an AU-ROC of 0.815-0.942. This suggests that our model can be applied broadly to other institutions without additional institution-specific fine-tuning.<\/jats:p>","DOI":"10.1101\/2023.06.26.23291912","type":"posted-content","created":{"date-parts":[[2023,6,27]],"date-time":"2023-06-27T15:45:26Z","timestamp":1687880726000},"source":"Crossref","is-referenced-by-count":3,"title":["Generalizable
